title: MS in Robotics - Khoury College of Computer Sciences
text:
MS in Robotics - Khoury College of Computer Sciences
MS in Robotics
Apply now
Request info
In the MS in Robotics — an interdisciplinary program between Khoury College and the
College of Engineering
— you’ll investigate the science and engineering of robotics. Through rigorous courses and hands-on experiences, you’ll gain a comprehensive understanding of the algorithms, sensors, control systems, and mechanisms of robotics as you prepare to make a transformational impact on society.
Explore program requirements in the course catalog
Program highlights
Location
Boston, Seattle
Credit hours
32 total semester hours required
Schedule
Core courses: Fall, Spring
Course format
In person
Take your career to the next level
Career prospects: A wealth of opportunities
Career prospects: A wealth of opportunities
This program will prepare you for diverse and rewarding career opportunities in the rapidly evolving field of robotics. Some careers of recent graduates:
Software support systems at Amazon Robotics
Autonomy and robotics software engineer for Aerospace Applications at MORSE Corporation
Robotics and control software engineer at Optimus Ride
Software integrator at Amazon Robotics
Co-ops and internships: Rated #1
Co-ops and internships: Rated #1
Through our renowned co-op program, ranked #1 in the country by U.S. News & World Report, you’ll have opportunities to apply your skills and knowledge in industry settings while you build invaluable connections. Recent positions held by co-op students include:
Robotics software engineering intern
Advanced robotics co-op
Machine learning co-op: natural language processing
UAV/robotics engineering co-op
Mobile robotics software engineer
Learn more about co-ops and internships
Research opportunities: Pursue your passion
Research opportunities: Pursue your passion
You’ll collaborate with leading researchers at top-tier facilities, exploring big questions and playing an important role in the discoveries of tomorrow. Whether you’re interested in a research career, applying to PhD programs, or simply building your expertise through hands-on experience, our diverse range of research projects will give you opportunities to make meaningful contributions.
Explore research at Khoury College
Choose your concentration
Choose your concentration
Your concentration enables you to specialize your master’s degree to fit your academic and professional goals. You can choose from:
Mechanical Engineering
Electrical and Computer Engineering
Computer Science
Learning outcomes
Learning outcomes
The MS in Robotics will prepare you to:
Use basic engineering concepts flexibly in a variety of contexts.
Build an interdisciplinary skill set that can evolve over time to suit your changing research and career interests, as well as an ever-evolving industry.
Campus locations
Boston
Located in the heart of Boston, Northeastern’s oldest campus is home to a vibrant community of students, researchers, and faculty working across disciplines to build a better world through CS.
Explore Boston's campus
Seattle
The Seattle campus partners with industry, government, and academia to bring programs in high-growth fields to the Pacific Northwest — becoming a valuable part of the region’s economy in the process.
Explore Seattle's campus
Applications and admissions
How to apply
How to apply
Our Master’s Apply page has everything you need to know about applying to the MS in Robotics program at Khoury college, including:
Master’s admissions requirements
Program specific admissions requirements
Timelines and deadlines
Resources and contact information
Learn about applying to Khoury College
Financial support
Financial support
Pursuing a master’s degree is a significant investment in your future. Northeastern University and Khoury College of Computer Sciences are here to help support you in this process. From financial aid to scholarship opportunities to co-op placements and teaching assistantships, there are multiple options to help fund your degree as you work towards your goals.
Learn more about financial support
Degree costs
Degree costs
Visit Student Financial Services for current tuition and fee information, and to find tools and calculators to help you estimate what your degree (32 credit hours) might cost.
Visit Student Financial Services
Deadlines
Domestic
Fall semester: August 1
Spring semester: December 1
International
Fall semester: April 15
Spring semester: September 15
Meet our faculty
Get to know the engaged, experienced professors who are here to support you every step of the way.
View all faculty
Robert Platt
Rob Platt is an associate professor at Khoury College, affiliated with the College of Engineering. He researches at the intersection of robotic perception, planning, and control, with the aim of enabling robots to perform manipulation tasks in home, factory, warehouse, military, and health care environments.
Read bio
No tech background? Check out the Align program
If you don’t have a background in computer science, then the Align MS in Computer Science might be for you. This program offers a direct path to a master’s degree — no prior tech experience required.
Learn about the Align program
Visit the College of Engineering
The MS in Robotics is an interdisciplinary collaboration with the College of Engineering.
Learn about the College of Engineering

The MS in Artificial Intelligence at Khoury College provides a comprehensive framework of theory and practice in the emerging field of AI.
